title: Hamiltonian Simulation with QSVT
subtitle: Hamiltonian Simulation with Quantum Singular Value Transformation
description: Implementation of time-independent Hamiltonian simulation via the Quantum Singular Value Transformation (QSVT) method. The algorithm decomposes the time evolution as cos(Ht) - i*sin(Ht), approximates each function separately as Chebyshev polynomials via the Jacobi–Anger expansion, and combines them via an optimized LCU using the qsvt_lcu function. QSVT achieves optimal query complexity without controlled block-encoding calls, using two auxiliary qubits.
